text: The Dataset Explorer now includes cohort-family review fields to help users avoid over-counting related accessions, assay layers, trial subsets, and integrated-resource components. All 102 dataset rows now have a study_family label, representing 85 distinct study or source families. The new detail fields are study_family, component_of_dataset_id, count_as_independent_cohort, and overlap_notes.

count_as_independent_cohort=FALSE means the row should not be counted as a separate cohort because it is a known component, subset, or overlapping accession for another row. Unclear means the row is related to another study family, but public metadata are not sufficient to reconcile donors or samples confidently. Users should treat Unclear rows conservatively and avoid summing them without source-level review.

Users can search by study, cohort, or trial names such as PROTECT, RISK, IBD200, ACT1, UNITI, UNIFI, GEMINI, BERGAMOT, scIBD, or SP140. These labels are intended for discoverability and cohort accounting; they do not replace direct verification of the source repository before analysis. Current countability values are 81 TRUE, 5 FALSE, and 16 Unclear.
